message:
test(conformance): differential harness for math.h core vs glibc

CONFORMANCE: libc math.h core diff matrix.

15 functions covered, 9 tests:

Exact-equality (0 ULP tolerance):
- sqrt: 18 inputs incl. NaN domain (-1.0), DBL_MAX, ±0, ±Inf, NaN
- fabs: 11 inputs
- floor / ceil: 14 inputs each
- fmod: 9 (x, y) cases incl. divide-by-zero (NaN), Inf operands

ULP-tolerant (≤4 ULPs):
- sin / cos / tan: 14 inputs each (0, π/4, π/2, π, 2π, neg, large, ±Inf, NaN)
- atan2: 9 (y, x) cases
- exp: 13 inputs incl. ±700 near edge, overflow→+Inf, underflow→0
- log / log2 / log10: 11 inputs each incl. 0→-Inf, neg→NaN
- pow: 8 (x, y) cases incl. 0^0, 1^Inf, Inf^0, neg-base-frac-exp→NaN
- sinh / cosh / tanh: 10 inputs each

Custom comparator handles IEEE-754 classification parity (NaN, ±Inf,
±0, denormal, ±normal) for special values rather than bit-level
equality, since NaN payloads can vary between libm vendors. ULP
distance is signed-magnitude bit difference for finite same-sign
values.

Math functions declared via local extern blocks since the libc crate
doesn't re-export them.

0 divergences observed at 4-ULP tolerance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
